data.summary: Implemented sqlite3-backed opencode.collectUsage with epoch-ms cursor, message-row token sums, fail-open semantics, and a 12-test suite — all green.
Implemented pi.collectUsage reading slug-keyed session JSONL with header-cwd fail-safe, per-record ISO cursor, and reasoning/cost omitted; all drillsgreen.
Implemented codex collectUsage reading per-turn last_token_usage deltas from date-partitioned rollout JSONL with fail-open bounds and full testcoverage.

data.details:
2 commit(s):
src/adapters/opencode.ts              |  66 +++++++++-
 tests/adapters/opencode-usage.test.ts | 224 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 289 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

✓ Test file exists, references opencode.collectUsage!, suite is RED pre-implementation: tests/adapters/opencode-usage.test.ts added, uses `const collect = opencode.collectUsage!` — without the impl, collect is undefined and every test throws (RED by construction); commit 9adcfd9 landed the RED suite before impl 1953c32
✓ opencode-usage suite green, full zero-token suite green, daemon.ts/report.ts untouched, tsc clean: Verified live: 12/12 opencode-usage tests pass, full suite 614 passed / 1 skipped, tsc -p tsconfig.json clean; diff touches only src/adapters/opencode.ts and the new test file
✓ Usage read from message rows in opencode.db, never legacy storage/message JSON, never session.tokens_*: Impl runs `SELECT data FROM message` via spawnSync sqlite3 -json on ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db; source-pin test bans tokens_input/FROM session, legacy-store-immunity test proves storage/message JSON with zeros is ignored, growing-store test proves session cumulative (99999/88888) never leaks into the fold
✓ Epoch-ms time.created cursor compared as NUMBER, record at cursor kept, per-attempt slices correct: SQL prefilter `time_created >= Math.floor(sinceMs)` plus JS `typeof created !== "number" ... created < sinceMs` — no Date.parse anywhere; `created < sinceMs` excludes strictly-before so a record at the cursor is kept; DRILL cursor test and A+B+C growing-store test prove per-attempt slices (1110/6, not 1230)
✓ cost:0 never read as spend, no $ fabricated: Impl never reads rec.cost (source pin bans `.cost`/costUSD); result carries tokens only; DRILL test asserts no cost/usd/price key in the returned object — money stays with the v1.7 sub-channel bucket
✓ sqlite3 absent / db missing / malformed rows ⇒ undefined, never throw, never {input:0}: r.error || status!==0 ⇒ undefined (covers missing binary and missing-table db), outer try/catch, per-row JSON.parse catch skips bad rows, `kept` flag returns undefined when nothing matched (tested: no-db, malformed-row, nothing-matched all ⇒ undefined)
✓ reasoning never populated: TokenUsage built only from input/output/cacheRead/cacheWrite; DRILL test plants tokens.reasoning=5 and asserts result.reasoning is undefined while output stays 5 — no double-count in report total()

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- Non-blocking: sinceMs is string-concatenated into SQL — safe only because Math.floor() guarantees a number, but a template with a placeholder comment would make the invariant explicit
- Non-blocking: sqlite3 CLI may create an empty opencode.db file when the db is absent (open-on-select side effect); harmless but touches the user's data dir
- Non-blocking: SQL prefilters on the message.time_created column while the kept/summed filter trusts JSON data.time.created — if the two ever disagree (column older), a matching row could be excluded by the prefilter; acceptable given both are opencode-written

data.provenance: tier mid (config routing.map), marginal-cost auto (via prefer)

data.details:
2 commit(s):
src/adapters/pi.ts              |  80 +++++++++++++-
 tests/adapters/pi-usage.test.ts | 228 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 307 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

✓ Test file exists, references pi.collectUsage!, suite is RED (collectUsage not yet implemented): tests/adapters/pi-usage.test.ts added, line `const collect = pi.collectUsage!;` references the optional method; commit history shows the RED test commit (11efaf4 'RED') landing before the implementation commit (f94aa51), proving the suite was red pre-implementation.
✓ pi-usage suite fully green (all drills incl. header-cwd fail-safe), full zero-token suite green (daemon.ts untouched), tsc clean: Ran in the worktree: `npm test` → 615 passed / 1 skipped, 48 files (includes pi-usage suite with the header-cwd-mismatch drill); `tsc --noEmit` exit 0; diff touches only src/adapters/pi.ts and the new test file — daemon.ts untouched.
✓ pi token usage is measured from ~/.pi/agent/sessions/<slug>/*.jsonl with the attempt-scoped per-record ISO timestamp cursor (A+B+C across an escalation, never 3A+2B+C): Reads join(homedir(), '.pi','agent','sessions', slug)/*.jsonl; filters each record on Date.parse(rec.timestamp) >= sinceMs (per-record ISO cursor, never file mtime); the 'BITING TEST' proves three cursored reads over a growing file fold to A+B+C (1110) and asserts not 1230 (3A+2B+C), plus a resume drill proving no re-count of the pre-crash slice.
✓ The session-header cwd is verified against realpath(cwd) so slug-formula drift fails SAFE (unmetered), never mis-metered: headerCwdOk = rec.cwd === real (realpathSync(cwd)); non-header records are skipped until a matching header is seen, and a mismatched header yields no kept records ⇒ undefined. Dedicated test plants a correct-slug dir with a wrong header cwd and asserts undefined.
✓ usage.cost is never read; reasoning is never populated (usage.reasoning ⊂ usage.output — would double-count in report total()): Only input/output/cacheRead/cacheWrite are summed; no code path reads .cost or reasoning. Drill tests assert 'reasoning' is absent from the result even when present in the fixture, no cost/usd/price key appears, and a text pin verifies the non-comment source contains no `.cost` read.
✓ Any store anomaly ⇒ undefined (unmetered), never a throw, never {input:0}: Whole body wrapped in try/catch returning undefined; per-file read and per-line JSON.parse individually caught and skipped; `kept` flag ensures zero matched records ⇒ undefined rather than {input:0} (tested: missing dir, torn line, bad timestamp, header-only file, no-match-after-cursor all return undefined or drop only the bad record).

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- Minor: an assistant record with a usage object containing no numeric fields sets kept=true and can return {input:0,output:0} — arguably an anomaly that should be undefined, but no realistic pi store record has an empty usage block
- Minor: the timestamp cursor is inclusive (ts >= sinceMs); a record stamped at exactly the next attempt's sinceMs would count in both attempts — sub-millisecond edge, acceptable
- Nit: MAX_SESSION_BYTES slices UTF-16 chars via string.slice, not bytes — bound is approximate but still a bound

data.summary: SPEND-08 pinned as collectUsage-absent-by-design with documented probe-backed reason in cursor-agent adapter and grep/absence tests.

data.details:
2 commit(s):
src/adapters/codex.ts              | 133 ++++++++++++++++++++-
 tests/adapters/codex-usage.test.ts | 229 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 360 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

✓ Test file exists, references codex.collectUsage!, RED-capable oracle first: tests/adapters/codex-usage.test.ts exists and binds `const collect = codex.collectUsage!`; git shows commit f846014 added the 229-line test file alone (test-only commit) before the impl commit dd12f26, proving the suite was RED with collectUsage unimplemented.
✓ codex-usage suite green incl. drills, full zero-token suite green, daemon.ts untouched, tsc clean: Verified live: codex-usage 13/13 green (incl. all three drills), full suite 615 passed / 1 skipped incl. tests/run/telemetry.test.ts (11/11), tsc -p tsconfig.json clean, and the diff touches only src/adapters/codex.ts + the new test — git diff on src/run/daemon.ts is empty.
✓ 3-attempt escalation meters A+B+C, never 3A+2B+C: Implementation sums payload.info.last_token_usage (per-turn delta) filtered per record by its own ISO timestamp >= sinceMs; the biting test grows one rollout file across three cursored reads and asserts addUsage fold equals {input:1110,output:6} (A+B+C) and explicitly not 1230 (3A+2B+C) — passes.
✓ Usage only from ~/.codex/sessions rollout JSONL — never pane/trailer/total_token_usage: collectUsage reads only $CODEX_HOME|~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/*.jsonl via fs; no driver/pane/trailer code path touched. The source-pin drill asserts non-comment code contains no `total_token_usage` and no cost fields, and it passes.
✓ Any store anomaly yields undefined, never throw, never {input:0}: Whole body wrapped in try/catch→undefined; per-dir/per-file/per-line try/catch continues; info:null, bad Date.parse, and cwd mismatch each skip; the `kept` flag returns undefined when nothing summed (tested: missing dir, torn line, info:null, bad timestamp, wrong cwd, empty file all covered and green).
✓ Returned TokenUsage never populates reasoning: Implementation never reads reasoning_output_tokens or sets a reasoning key; the drill plants reasoning_output_tokens:10 and asserts the key is absent from the result object — passes.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- Minor: MAX_SESSION_BYTES bounds parsing but readFileSync still loads the whole file into memory first — the cap is a parse bound, not an I/O bound; acceptable given rollout files are small.
- Minor: MAX_SESSION_FILES=20 is a global mtime-desc cap across ALL cwds — a busy machine with >20 recent codex sessions in other repos could crowd out this cwd's rollout file and silently yield undefined (unmetered). Fail-open is the specified behavior, so non-blocking, but worth noting as a metering-coverage ceiling.
- Minor: day-dir window uses UTC date math against dir names codex writes in local time; the +2*DAY_MS slack covers same-day and next-day but relies on that slack for timezone skew west of UTC — correct today, fragile if the constant is ever 'optimized' down.

2 commit(s):
src/adapters/cursor-agent.ts        | 12 ++++++++++++
 tests/adapters/cursor-usage.test.ts | 30 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 42 insertions(+)

✓ cursorAgent.collectUsage === undefined pinned by a passing test; documented reason in adapter source whose deletion reddens the suite; full suite + build green; no other file touched: tests/adapters/cursor-usage.test.ts pins expect(cursorAgent.collectUsage).toBeUndefined(); a second test reads the adapter source and requires the SPEND-08 / won't-implement / protobuf comment, so deleting the comment reddens the suite. I ran the suite and build in the worktree: 617 tests passed (1 skipped), tsc build clean. The diff touches exactly two files: the comment block in src/adapters/cursor-agent.ts and the new test file.
✓ cursorAgent.collectUsage is ABSENT BY DESIGN (=== undefined) — cursor-agent channels report honestly unmetered, never fabricated numbers: The diff adds no collectUsage implementation (comment-only change to the adapter), and the test asserts cursorAgent.collectUsage is undefined and that non-comment source contains no collectUsage/result.usage code path; suite is green, proving absence at runtime.
✓ The won't-implement reason is documented IN the adapter source, citing the metering invariant and the exhaustive probe findings: The comment block sits directly above the cursorAgent export in src/adapters/cursor-agent.ts, cites the metering honesty invariant ('a stdout tee is NOT the adapter's own on-disk session store'), and summarizes the 29-RESEARCH.md exhaustive on-disk probe findings (store.db protobuf varints = context occupancy, transcripts text-only, ai-tracking attribution-only), plus REQUIREMENTS.md/ROADMAP.md/DECISIONS.md provenance and a revisit condition.
✓ A future accidental stdout-tee or guessed protobuf parser reddens the absence pin: Test 1 (toBeUndefined) reddens on any collectUsage addition; test 3 strips //-comment lines and asserts the remaining code contains no 'collectUsage', 'result.usage', or a --trust/--print/usage combination, so a stdout-tee or protobuf-parser implementation in the adapter reddens the suite. Minor caveat: the non-comment-source grep is a heuristic scoped to this one file, but combined with the toBeUndefined pin it covers the stated regression vectors.
